title: oryctanthus grandis
intro: Oryctanthus grandis, also known as the Devil's Claw, is a flowering plant native to southern Africa, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects due to the presence of harpagoside, a unique compound found in its roots. The plant is known to provide various health benefits, such as relieving arthritis, back pain, and other musculoskeletal issues, as well as aiding in digestion and improving sleep quality. Oryctanthus grandis is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in various herbal remedies, including creams and ointments.
